IN DEPT. 302, LAW & MOTION CALENDAR. JUDGE PRO TEM: SANFORD KINGSLEY, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S AMENDED RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION (ADDED FOR POSTING OF TENTATIVE RULING) HEARING SET FOR JUL-30-2015 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-30-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-30-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER RE: MOTION TO COMPEL

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MARY RYAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES AFTER CONSIDERATION OF THE MOVING PAPERS AND SAFEWAY INC.'S OPPOSITION THERETO AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HER ATTORNEY RICHARD FRISCHER AND GOOD CAUSE APPEARING, THE COURT ENTERS THE FOLLOWING ORDER: REQUEST NO. 4: DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE COPIES OF ALL CUSTOMER INCIDENT REPORTS WITH THE CUSTOMER'S NAME, ADDRESS AND TELEPHONE NUMBER REDACTED, FOR ALL SLIP OR TRIP INCIDENTS ON THE CUSTOMER SALES FLOOR OF THE STORE FOR THE PERIOD THREE YEARS PRIOR TO THE FALL TO THE DATE OF THE FALL. SAFEWAY SHALL PROVIDE A PRIVILEGE LOG FOR ANY ACCIDENT REPORTS PREPARED BY SAFEWAY FOR SLIP OR TRIP INCIDENTS FOR THE SAME TIME PERIOD. REQUEST NO. 5: DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE ALL REPORTS OF COMPLAINTS CONCERNING THE CONDITION OF THE CUSTOMER SALES FLOOR OF THE STORE FOR THE PERIOD 3 YEARS PRIOR TO THE FALL TO THE DATE OF THE FALL. REQUEST NO. 6: DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE ALL DOCUMENTS WHICH DESCRIBE INSPECTION OR MAINTENANCE OF THE FLOOR OF THE STORE FOR A PERIOD OF THREE YEARS PRIOR TO THE DATE OF THE INCIDENT AND WHICH WERE IN EFFECT ON THE DATE OF THE INCIDENT. REQUEST NO. 10: DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE THE SWEEP LOGS FOR 30 DAYS PRIOR TO THE DATE OF THE INCIDENT. ORDER WAS SIGNED PER THE AGREEMENT OF COUNSEL IN ITS AMENDED RESPONSES TO REQUEST NOS. 4, 5, 6, AND 10, DEFENDANT SHALL STATE THAT AFTER DILIGENT SEARCH AND REASONABLE INQUIRY, ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS HAVE BEEN PRODUCED. ORDER SIGNED IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.
LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S AMENDED RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION IS A DUPLICATE ENTRY.

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. RICHARD L. FRISCHER, ESQ. of Lamb & Frischer Law Firm LLP, Tel No. 415-997-9529 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. LAUREN E. TATE, ESQ. of Tate & Associates, Tel No. 510-525-5100 appearing for the Defendant Safeway, Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAFEWAY, INC.'S motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to maintain its burden of production. First, Defendant's employee (Ms. Deng) cleaned up a mess at aisle 2 at 11:48 a.m. and a reasonable trier of fact could conclude that her failure to adequately clean the mess caused Plaintiff's injury. (Deng depo, 42:16 - 44:7). Second, there is a triable issue of material fact whether defendant had constructive notice. In Ortega v. K-Mart Corp. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 1200, 1203, the court explains that where the source of the dangerous condition is unknown, a failure of the owner to inspect the premises within a reasonable time "is sufficient to allow an inference that the condition was on the floor long enough to give the owner the opportunity to discover and remedy it." Whether defendant's hourly inspection interval policy was adequate is a question for the trier of fact. (Nicholas Dec., 2:21-23). Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant'S Amended Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Jul-30-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant'S Amended Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production IS A DUPLICATE ENTRY.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant'S Amended Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production
Off Calendar Jul-30-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. Plaintiff MARY RYAN's Motion to Compel Further Responses After consideration of the moving papers and SAFEWAY INC.'s Opposition thereto and Request for Sanctions against plaintiff and her attorney Richard Frischer and good cause appearing, the Court enters the following Order: Request No. 4: Defendant shall provide copies of all Customer Incident Reports with the customer's name, address and telephone number redacted, for all slip or trip incidents on the customer sales floor of the store for the period three years prior to the fall to the date of the fall. Safeway shall provide a privilege log for any Accident Reports prepared by Safeway for slip or trip incidents for the same time period.
Request No. 5: Defendant shall provide all reports of complaints concerning the
condition of the customer sales floor of the store for the period 3 years prior to the fall to the date of the fall.
Request No. 6: Defendant shall provide all documents which describe inspection or maintenance of the floor of the store for a period of three years prior to the date of the incident and which were in effect on the date of the incident.
Request No. 10: Defendant shall provide the Sweep Logs for 30 days prior to the date of the incident. Order was signed per the agreement of counsel
In its amended responses to Request Nos. 4, 5, 6, and 10, defendant shall state that after diligent search and reasonable inquiry, all responsive documents have been produced.
Order signed in accordance with the agreement of the parties. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported.
